description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly dark gray coloration and a rough, uneven surface texture. The rock features a wedge-like profile, with one end tapering to a point and the opposite end more squared and blocky. Its surface is mottled with lighter gray and bluish speckles, giving it a natural, weathered appearance. One side of the rock displays a visible marking or writing, suggesting some human interaction or cataloging. The rock's edges are jagged and fractured, with no smooth or polished faces, indicating it has not been artificially shaped. Its overall form and texture make it suitable as a natural obstacle or barrier in an urban environment such as a park or landscaped area.
description: A dark gray, wedge-shaped rock with rough, jagged surfaces and visible speckling, featuring a human-made marking on one side.
description_view_0: The rock appears as a broad, slanted wedge with a rough, mottled surface and visible texture variations.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock looks almost rectangular with a flat top and a rough, uneven edge on the left.
description_view_2: This side highlights the pointed, triangular profile of the rock, showing a smoother, less textured face.
description_view_3: The rock's squared end is visible, featuring a clear marking or writing and a blocky, fractured surface.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
